narrative: I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimated an 88% chance you'll fail the bar exam on your first try, based on patterns from thousands of past students. Your profile includes being a full-time male student from a middle-income family with a Native American background, an undergraduate GPA of 1.8, an LSAT score of 25, and ranking in the lowest decile for both your first-year and third-year school grades—far below the typical student's averages of around 3.2 GPA, 37 on the LSAT, and mid-level deciles in the 5s. This prediction isn't a judgment on your potential, but a data-driven assessment comparing you to others who went on to take the bar. The strongest factor pulling the prediction toward failure was your third-year grade decile, as being in the bottom rung signals challenges in sustaining academic performance compared to peers. Right behind that was your LSAT score, which is notably lower than most applicants and has historically been a key hurdle for bar success. Your first-year grade decile ranked third in influence, reinforcing concerns about early academic struggles that often persist. Family income came next, with your middle bracket slightly tilting the odds downward relative to higher-earning families in the dataset. Your racial background as Native American followed, underrepresented in the data and linked to tougher outcomes for a small group of students. Even your undergraduate GPA, full-time status, and gender played smaller roles, all nudging the model toward expecting a fail rather than a pass. In essence, academic metrics like grades and test scores dominated the decision, far outweighing demographics or enrollment type. To boost your future chances, prioritize retaking the LSAT for a higher score through dedicated prep, as that's one of the most changeable factors. Simultaneously, focus on excelling in any remaining coursework to lift your deciles—stronger grades have flipped predictions for many students like you. With targeted effort here, you could realign your profile closer to successful bar passers and reapply stronger. Keep pushing; data shows improvement is possible.
